## Venue

- **Primary target**: Lifetime Data Analysis
- **Alternative**: Communications in Statistics — Theory and Methods
- **Rationale**: Expo companion (exponential FIM) submitted to LDA; this paper extends that work to Weibull. Same audience, natural pairing. Flehinger et al.'s masked data papers appeared in LDA.

## Thesis

This paper provides the complete Weibull treatment for masked series systems: explicit score equations, Fisher information matrices, comprehensive simulation studies, and practical estimation guidance for both homogeneous (common shape) and heterogeneous (distinct shapes) Weibull components.

## Phase

- **Current**: `draft` (v0.1.0 — 8 pages, compiles clean)
- **Next action**: Internal review; refine MC table values; consider simulation study
- **Blocking**: None

## Outline

1. Introduction — gap, contributions, organization
2. Model — rate parametrization, K⊥T independence, log-likelihood
3. Fisher information — score equations, Theorem 1 (closed-form FIM), special cases
4. Masking invariance — Theorem 2 (shape info invariant), masked FIM structure, info loss
5. Numerical verification — MC with 10⁶ replications, special case checks
6. Discussion — applications, extensions (censoring, heterogeneous), software

| 6 | Information loss decomposition (masked vs complete) | **proved** (notes/fim-derivation.md) |

## What Already Exists

- `maskedcauses` R package implements all three model levels (exponential, homogeneous, heterogeneous)
- `vignettes/framework.Rmd` walks through the theory with code
- Model selection paper has extensive LRT simulations for hom/het comparison
- expo-masked-fim has the exponential Fisher information

## Foundation Paper Citation

```bibtex
@article{towell2025masked,
  author  = {Towell, Alexander},
  title   = {Masked Causes of Failure in Series Systems: A Likelihood Framework},
  year    = {2025},
  doi     = {10.5281/zenodo.18725577},
  url     = {https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.18725577}
}
```

**Specific results cited:**
| Foundation Result | Used In (this paper) | How |
|-------------------|----------------------|-----|
| Conditions C1-C2-C3 (Sec 5) | Sec 2 | Framework assumptions |
| Combined likelihood (Theorem 9) | Sec 3-4 | Specialized to Weibull hazard |
| Theorem 10 (log-likelihood) | Sec 3-4 | Score equations derived from this |
| Identifiability Theorem | Sec 2 | Conditions under which Weibull MLE exists |
| Common Hazard Specifications — Weibull (Sec 7) | Sec 3-4 | Starting point; this paper develops the full MLE treatment |

**Relationship**: Heavy dependency — this paper instantiates the general framework for Weibull, deriving everything the foundation paper leaves abstract (score equations, FIM, asymptotics, simulations).

## Related Work (Ecosystem)

### Expo-Masked-FIM (`~/github/papers/expo-masked-fim/`)
- **Status**: Review (v3.2.0)
- **Role**: Exponential special case — provides the analytical baseline. This paper extends the FIM derivation approach from exponential to Weibull, where closed forms may still exist for the homogeneous case.

### Model Selection Paper (`~/github/papers/masked-series-model-selection/`)
- **Status**: Draft complete
- **Role**: Cross-reference for hom/het comparison (don't duplicate). LRT methodology and power analysis already done there. This paper focuses on estimation, not selection.

### Master's Project (`~/github/papers/reliability-estimation-in-series-systems/`)
- **Role**: Origin. Weibull simulation infrastructure established there.

### Foundation Paper (`~/github/papers/masked-causes-in-series-systems/`)
- **Role**: Heavy dependency. Score equations derived from the general log-likelihood (Theorem 10).

### Sibling Companions
- **identifiability-info-loss**: FIM decomposition framework applies to Weibull case.
- **nesting-vs-structure**: This paper provides the estimation theory for the upper nesting levels that paper discusses methodologically.

### R Packages
- `maskedcauses` (v0.9): Implements all three model levels (expo, hom-Weibull, het-Weibull). Score, Hessian, and MLE already coded.
- `compositional.mle` (v1.0.2): Numerical optimization for heterogeneous Weibull MLE.

## Open Questions

- ~~Closed-form Fisher information for homogeneous Weibull?~~ **RESOLVED**: Yes, closed form in (k, λ) parametrization using γ and π²/6 only. See notes/fim-derivation.md.
- Closed-form FIM for heterogeneous Weibull? (Likely no — K ⊥ T fails, moments involve integrals without closed form)
- Profile likelihood for shape parameter under heavy masking?
- Bootstrap vs asymptotic confidence intervals: when does asymptotic break down?
- Optimal starting values for Weibull MLE under masking?

## Log

- **2026-02-17**: Initialized papermill. Idea stage; high effort — full derivations, simulation study, and case study needed.
- **2026-02-18**: Added foundation paper citation block. Heavy dependency — instantiates general framework for Weibull (score eqs, FIM, asymptotics).
- **2026-02-26**: **FIM gate resolved.** Derived closed-form per-observation Fisher information matrix for homogeneous Weibull series system in (k, λ) parametrization. Result: I_{kk} = μ² + π²/(6k²), I_{λ_j,λ_l} = δ_{jl}/(λ_j·λ_sys), I_{k,λ_j} = μ/λ_sys where μ = log β_sys + (1-γ)/k. Numerically verified (Monte Carlo, n=10⁶, max relative error 0.44%). Key bonus results: (1) masking invariance of shape information, (2) masked FIM = unmasked FIM with expo-masked-fim λ-block substitution, (3) info loss from masking confined to rate block.
- **2026-02-26**: **Draft v0.1.0 written.** 8-page surgical paper: 2 theorems (closed-form FIM + masking invariance), 1 corollary (info loss decomposition), MC verification table, 2 special case checks. Venue: Lifetime Data Analysis.
